checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=145,563,000 (2012-01-28) grew to 174,484,000 (2013-02-02), +19.9%, while SalesRevenueNet fell from 955,549,000 (FY ended 2012-01-28) to 942,451,000 (FY ended 2013-02-02), -1.4%
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=137,432,000 (2015-01-31) declined -6.1% from 146,392,000 (2014-02-01), roughly tracking SalesRevenueNet decline of -2.76% (882,573,000 to 858,237,000)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=246,681,000 (2013-02-02) declined to 211,485,000 (2014-02-01), then remained roughly flat through 206,781,000 (2015-05-02)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=302,788,000 on SalesRevenueNet=882,573,000 (FY2013, 34.3% margin) vs GrossProfit=303,004,000 on SalesRevenueNet=858,237,000 (FY2014, 35.3% margin)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filing_date 1996-12-17; NT 10-Q filing_date 1997-09-16; NT 10-K filing_date 2004-04-16; NT 10-K filing_date 2008-04-16
Cluster of DFAN14A, PREC14A, DEFA14A, SC 13D/A filings between 2015-05-08 and 2015-05-29

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available from fiscal year ended 2011-01-29 through quarter ended 2015-05-02 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, InventoryNet, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, and cash flow statement lines

top_signals: Persistent and direction-flipping divergence between NetIncomeLoss and operating cash flow across FY2011-FY2014 (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=25,517,000 vs OCF=712,000 in FY2011; NetIncomeLoss=-37,175,000 vs OCF=55,143,000 in FY2014), partly explained by non-cash impairment add-backs but still notable for earnings-quality assessment.

Declining allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables (from ~19.1% at 2012-01-28 to ~15.1% at 2015-01-31) during a period of revenue contraction and rising losses, which could indicate under-reserving.

Inventory growth (+12.8%, from 183,127,000 to 206,602,000) materially outpacing revenue decline (-6.3%) in the fiscal year ended 2014-02-01, raising obsolescence/write-down risk not yet reflected in reported gross margins.

Large goodwill and intangible asset impairments recorded in FY ended 2014-02-01 (Goodwill 13,794,000 -> 6,022,000; intangibles 246,681,000 -> 211,485,000) are a mitigating factor, showing the company was recognizing declining asset values rather than concealing them, which reduces (but does not eliminate) overall misstatement risk.

No NT filings, late filings, or 10-K/A amendments occurred within the 2011-2015 period covered by the financial data; the 2015 surge in 8-K/proxy filings reflects an activist shareholder campaign rather than accounting irregularities.
